articles.title: Kawhi mum on future, 'not stressing' NBA probe
articles.original_title: Kawhi mum on future, 'not stressing' NBA probe
articles.summary: Kawhi Leonard declined to discuss his future with the Clippers after Wednesday's play-in loss and said he's "not stressing" the NBA's investigation into his deal with Aspiration.

articles.title: Fantasy Basketball Exit Interviews: What's next for the 10 teams that missed the NBA playoffs?
articles.original_title: Fantasy Basketball Exit Interviews: What's next for the 10 teams that missed the NBA playoffs?
articles.summary: Fantasy basketball analyst Dan Titus takes a look at teams eliminated from the playoffs and what's next this offseason.

articles.title: Hawks rule out backup center Jock Landale for start of playoff series against the Knicks
articles.original_title: Hawks rule out backup center Jock Landale for start of playoff series against the Knicks
articles.summary: Atlanta Hawks center Jock Landale will miss at least the first two weeks of the NBA playoffs because of his sprained right ankle.  Landale averaged 9.1 points and 4.1 rebounds in 23 regular-season games with Atlanta.  The Hawks say he “is progressing in his rehabilitation” and “will be re-evaluated in approximately two weeks.”

articles.title: Nuggets center Nikola Jokic surges into playoffs after a scary knee injury and historic season
articles.original_title: Nuggets center Nikola Jokic surges into playoffs after a scary knee injury and historic season
articles.summary: Around playoff time, Nikola Jokic always looks to make a big splash — on the court, obviously, but also in the water.  The Denver Nuggets do-it-all center typically opens his swimming pool at this point of the year — weather permitting — for he and his kids to have some fun.  Turns out, floating around in between lofting floaters is the perfect way for him to take a break from the pressure of leading the team on a title chase.

articles.title: 'A bigger shock to the system than we acknowledged': How 48 hours changed the Lakers' season
articles.original_title: 'A bigger shock to the system than we acknowledged': How 48 hours changed the Lakers' season
articles.summary: After a devastating blowout loss in Oklahoma City on April 2, the Lakers lost two of their three stars. Now they face Kevin Durant and the Houston Rockets -- as a home underdog.

articles.title: Vintage Curry takes over as Dubs extend season
articles.original_title: Vintage Curry takes over as Dubs extend season
articles.summary: The Warriors, led by Stephen Curry, erased a 13-point deficit in the fourth quarter to edge the Clippers and advance in the NBA's play-in tournament.

articles.title: Minus Embiid, Maxey propels Sixers into playoffs
articles.original_title: Minus Embiid, Maxey propels Sixers into playoffs
articles.summary: With Joel Embiid watching from the bench, Tyrese Maxey scored 31 points to lead the Sixers past the Magic 109-97 and lock up a first-round playoff date with the rival Boston Celtics.

articles.title: Hornets' Ball fined $35K for 'reckless' Bam play
articles.original_title: Hornets' Ball fined $35K for 'reckless' Bam play
articles.summary: The NBA has fined LaMelo Ball $35,000 "for making unnecessary and reckless contact" with Bam Adebayo during Tuesday's play-in game. The Hornets star also was fined $25,000 for using profane language during a TV interview after that game.

articles.title: Wolves ready to 'flip the switch' off uneven season
articles.original_title: Wolves ready to 'flip the switch' off uneven season
articles.summary: Coming off a rather uninspiring regular season, Anthony Edwards and the Timberwolves said "we're here now" and are ready to take on the Nuggets for the start of the playoffs.

articles.summary: Referee Ben Taylor left the play-in tournament game between the Golden State Warriors and the Los Angeles Clippers because of an injury.
